title: Data-Efficient Finetuning Using Cross-Task Nearest Neighbors

structured.summary: The paper introduces Data-Efficient FineTuning (DEFT), a method that improves finetuning by selecting relevant training instances from a multitask dataset pool. DEFT uses similarity-based retrieval to enhance model performance and reduce data usage compared to existing multitask language models like T0-3B.

strengths.evidence: DEFT models outperform the T0-3B model while using only 2% of the training data, achieving performance improvements of 8-30%.
DEFT models show a 3-23% improvement over few-shot finetuned T0-3B models on 8 out of 9 datasets.

weaknesses.evidence: The methodology mentions using distance between representations but does not compare alternatives or justify the selected metric.
The paper evaluates DEFT primarily against T0-3B without comparison to other retrieval-based or data-efficient methods.

questions.question: What specific similarity measure was used and why was it chosen over others?
questions.motivation: Understanding the selection criteria for similarity measures is crucial to evaluate the retrieval step's efficacy.

critique_points.point_001: Limited exploration of similarity measures. The methodology mentions using distance between representations but does not compare alternatives or justify the selected metric
critique_points.point_002: Lack of comparative evaluation. The paper evaluates DEFT primarily against T0-3B without comparison to other retrieval-based or data-efficient methods
